global.title: BMW i3 Touring caught for the first time, and that wagon rear is hard to miss
global.summary: After the sedan version launched last month, the new BMW i3 Touring was captured for the first time with its wagon tail on display.

global.title: Podcast: TSLA shareholders wake up, Xpeng targets EU, VW goes cheaper, and more
global.summary: In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Tesla shareholders potentially starting to wake up, Xpeng targeting the European market, VW going cheaper with its EVs, and more.

global.title: Toyota is offering $3,000 if you trade in your old EV for its new electric SUV
global.summary: Toyota’s new bZ electric SUV is nearly outselling the Prius this year. With an extra $3,000 off if you trade in your old EV in California, it might be worth checking out.

global.title: EcoFlow brings back Prime Day power station pricing in sale from $169, Segway Xafari e-bike $1,550 low, EGO Multi-Head, more
global.summary: We’re closing out this week’s Green Deals with an EV-heavy edition, though our headliner is EcoFlow’s second phase Outdoor Season Sale with returning Prime Day Pricing on power stations + multiple FREE gift tiers – all starting from $169. Right behind that, we have Segway’s Xafari All-Terrain Fat Ti

global.title: In the birthplace of the car, EVs are now king: BEVs outsell gas cars in Germany
global.summary: For the first time, battery electric vehicles were the most popular powertrain in Germany in June, setting a new sales record as the birthplace of the automobile moves forward into the next stage of its evolution.

global.title: BYD unveils its latest luxury EV with sleek fastback styling and 570 miles of range
global.summary: BYD revealed the new Denza Z9S, a high-tech luxury electric sedan that can drive over 570 miles (920 km) on a single charge.

global.title: How getting a Garmin watch changed my e-biking (and my fitness lifestyle)
global.summary: I’m not usually the kind of person who gets excited about wearing a watch. But I am apparently the kind of person who paces around my living room at a quarter to midnight because a tiny computer on my wrist informed me I was 200 steps short of today’s goal. So here we are, with the story of how I go

global.title: Volkswagen’s new ID. Polo EV secured 25,000 orders in weeks — Now, it’s even cheaper
global.summary: Volkswagen opened pre-orders for the ID. Polo with the smaller 37 kWh battery, starting at under $30,000. The lower-priced model arrives after Volkswagen’s new EV received 25,000 orders weeks after it went on sale.

global.title: Tesla launches in Latvia and Uruguay, expanding on two continents
global.summary: Tesla opened two new markets in less than 24 hours, launching in Latvia and Uruguay in back-to-back announcements this week. The dual entry completes Tesla’s expansion across all three Baltic states and gives the automaker its third market in South America — two very different markets that show how

global.title: Wheel-E Podcast: New Tern HSD, 65 MPH ‘e-bike’, Segway Xaber review, more
global.summary: This week on Electrek’s Wheel-E podcast, we discuss the most popular news stories from the world of electric bikes and other nontraditional electric vehicles. This time, that includes new e-bike launches from Tern, Trek, Momentum, and Ariel Rider, reviews of the Infinite Machine Olto and Segway Xabe
